_note: Subagent `description` is your host's dispatch signal — it must be trigger-phrased, not an identity label (Claude Code: YAML `description` frontmatter drives auto-dispatch; other hosts: see install.md). `model` is used where the host supports per-worker model selection (Claude Code: YAML `model` frontmatter). The lead `stig` runs as the main agent and uses the session's model.

agents.name: stig
agents.description: The lead. Installed into the host's lead-persona target, holds project context via Monet, and delegates to the workers below via the host's delegation primitive.

agents.name: explorer
agents.description: Use PROACTIVELY to investigate the codebase: locate files, symbols, call sites, and how a feature works, then report concise findings. Read-only — never edits.

agents.name: researcher
agents.description: Use PROACTIVELY when a decision needs external prior art, library docs, or web research the repo can't answer. Returns options and tradeoffs, not edits.

agents.name: analyst
agents.description: Use PROACTIVELY for architecture and planning under constraints: assess feasibility, risks, and the approach before implementation. Also use when product scope or acceptance criteria are unclear: it surfaces the ambiguity back to the lead to take to the user — it does not decide scope. Does not write code.

agents.name: mechanic
agents.description: Use PROACTIVELY for small, mechanical, low-risk changes: docs, comments, copy, config values, renames, single-file or tightly-scoped few-file diffs that don't need architectural judgment. Escalate to developer for anything bigger.

agents.name: developer
agents.description: Use PROACTIVELY to implement and refactor code, add tests, and run local validation, once the approach and the user's go-ahead are clear.

agents.name: tester
agents.description: Use PROACTIVELY to verify a change: design and run tests, reproduce regressions, and judge whether it actually works.

agents.name: reviewer
agents.description: Use PROACTIVELY after writing or changing code to review for correctness, security, maintainability, and basic operational risk (SLOs, retries, observability). Flags live incident or release-readiness concerns to the lead. Returns findings by severity; does not mutate PRs.

agents.name: auditor
agents.description: Use PROACTIVELY as the independent second review on substantive changes: audits the diff against the codebase with NO design context — data-model closure, sibling precedents, advertised contracts, invariant bypass, bounds, determinism. Pairs with reviewer (who audits against intent); brief it with only the diff location.

agents.name: security
agents.description: Use PROACTIVELY for trust-boundary, auth, secrets, or data-exposure concerns: threat-model and review for security risk.
